customers.name: Finance Operations Director / Group Controller
customers.description: Finance leader responsible for controlling employee spend, keeping travel and expense policy enforceable, reducing manual reimbursement and month-end work, and ensuring every spend record is audit-ready across multiple countries, entities, and ERP landscapes.
careAbout: Reducing manual review and reconciliation work
Keeping spend policy enforceable before and after purchase
Faster month-end close for employee expenses and card spend
Reliable ERP, accounting, and tax exports across entities
Audit-ready records with receipt, approver, and policy evidence
Controlling travel spend without creating employee frustration
winsThem: Fast mobile capture that increases submission compliance
Strong policy engine with smart audit and exception routing
Automatic matching of card events, receipts, and expense lines
Clean ERP-ready exports and entity-aware accounting controls
Spend analytics that explain leakage by team, merchant, and category
theirFear: Finance teams becoming the bottleneck for reimbursement and close
Hidden policy leakage in card and travel spend
Audit failures caused by incomplete records or inconsistent controls

jobsToBeDone.name: Control employee spend without drowning finance in manual work
jobsToBeDone.what_it_is: Operate travel, out-of-pocket, and card spend with enough automation that policy, approvals, and reconciliations stay under control as volume grows.
jobsToBeDone.outcome: Lower processing cost per expense, fewer policy breaches, and faster time from spend to booked accounting entry.

steps.step: Capture spend evidence as close to the transaction as possible
steps.description: Get employees to submit receipts, mileage, per diem, and card-backed expenses quickly enough that finance is not chasing missing information later.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Receipt Capture and OCR
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Extracts merchant, amount, date, currency, and tax fields from receipts so expense creation starts with structured data.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Mobile Expense Submission
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Lets employees submit expenses in the moment instead of batching them weeks later.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Mileage and Per Diem Engine
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Automates travel-specific expense calculation for car trips and daily allowances.

media.title: Finance Operations Director / Group Controller JTBD step 1: Capture spend evidence as close to the transaction as possible
media.alt: Enterprise infographic for step 1 of the job "Control employee spend without drowning finance in manual work": Capture spend evidence as close to the transaction as possible.

steps.step: Apply policy and route the right exceptions
steps.description: Automatically enforce spending thresholds, required fields, category rules, and budget ownership so only true exceptions reach finance.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Policy Rules Engine
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Applies configurable policy logic by entity, cost center, traveler, merchant, and spend type.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Smart Audit and Risk Scoring
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Flags duplicate, risky, out-of-policy, or suspicious expenses so finance reviews the smallest necessary set.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Approval Workflow Orchestration
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Routes exceptions and manager approvals using rules that fit the company structure.

media.title: Finance Operations Director / Group Controller JTBD step 2: Apply policy and route the right exceptions
media.alt: Enterprise infographic for step 2 of the job "Control employee spend without drowning finance in manual work": Apply policy and route the right exceptions.

steps.step: Reconcile card and reimbursement spend cleanly
steps.description: Match card transactions, receipts, and expense lines so finance can distinguish reimbursable, card-paid, and unresolved activity quickly.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Corporate Card Program
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Provides issued or connected card flows for controlled employee spend.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Card Transaction Reconciliation
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Matches card feeds with receipts and expense reports to reduce manual investigation.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Reimbursement Orchestration
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Turns approved out-of-pocket expenses into payable employee reimbursements with clear status.

media.title: Finance Operations Director / Group Controller JTBD step 3: Reconcile card and reimbursement spend cleanly
media.alt: Enterprise infographic for step 3 of the job "Control employee spend without drowning finance in manual work": Reconcile card and reimbursement spend cleanly.

steps.step: Post booked spend into finance systems
steps.description: Export approved and coded spend into ERP, HRIS, accounting, and tax workflows with entity and audit context intact.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: ERP and Accounting Sync
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Pushes approved expense data into general ledger, AP, and accounting systems in the right format.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: VAT and Tax Handling
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Supports tax extraction, tax coding, and recovery-friendly exports.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Audit Record Vault
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Preserves receipts, approvals, policy checks, and export evidence in one traceable record.

media.title: Finance Operations Director / Group Controller JTBD step 4: Post booked spend into finance systems
media.alt: Enterprise infographic for step 4 of the job "Control employee spend without drowning finance in manual work": Post booked spend into finance systems.

media.title: Finance Operations Director / Group Controller JTBD infographic: Control employee spend without drowning finance in manual work
media.alt: Enterprise infographic for the job "Control employee spend without drowning finance in manual work" for customer "Finance Operations Director / Group Controller".

jobsToBeDone.name: Give finance and auditors one trusted view of travel and expense controls
jobsToBeDone.what_it_is: Create a system of record for who spent what, why it was allowed, how it was approved, and how it mapped into accounting and policy.
jobsToBeDone.outcome: Faster audits, cleaner month-end, and higher confidence that spend controls hold across countries and teams.

steps.description: Make sure every expense record carries the same coding, entity, cost-center, and traveler context.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Spend Categorization and Tax Coding
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Normalizes categories, tax treatment, and accounting dimensions across expense types.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Budget and Cost Center Controls
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Connects spend records to budget owners and policy thresholds.

media.alt: Enterprise infographic for step 1 of the job "Give finance and auditors one trusted view of travel and expense controls": Standardize data and coding.

steps.description: Preserve enough evidence that finance can explain duplicates, policy overrides, and missing receipts without forensic work.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Smart Audit and Risk Scoring
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Maintains explainable flags and review context around suspect claims.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Audit Record Vault
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Keeps the complete approval, receipt, and correction history available for review.

media.title: Finance Operations Director / Group Controller JTBD step 2: Explain why exceptions happened
media.alt: Enterprise infographic for step 2 of the job "Give finance and auditors one trusted view of travel and expense controls": Explain why exceptions happened.

steps.description: Show which teams, travelers, merchants, or workflows are creating unnecessary cost and delay.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Spend Analytics and Benchmarking
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Surfaces policy leakage, processing time, spend concentration, and automation performance.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Anomaly and Leakage Detection
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Highlights unusual spend patterns and control gaps that deserve policy or vendor action.

media.alt: Enterprise infographic for step 3 of the job "Give finance and auditors one trusted view of travel and expense controls": Measure leakage and process quality.

stages.narrative: Month-end closes are slowed by missing receipts, unclear card spend, and high manual review effort.

top.name: Monthly close cycle for employee spend
top.description: Reduce the average time needed to close employee expense, travel, and card spend from period end to booked completion to 2.5 business days.

children.name: Policy-compliant expense rate
children.description: Increase the share of submitted expenses that pass policy without manual correction to 94%.

children.name: Expense submission within 48 hours
children.description: Increase the share of expenses submitted within 48 hours of spend to 88%.

children.name: Auto-matched card transaction rate
children.description: Increase the share of imported card transactions matched automatically to receipts or expenses to 87%.

children.name: Audit-ready record completeness
children.description: Increase the share of approved expense records with complete receipt, approver, and policy evidence to 98%.

top.name: Finance automation suite adoption
top.description: Increase the share of strategic accounts using expense, cards, policy automation, and finance integrations together to 46%.

children.name: Net revenue retention in enterprise accounts
children.description: Keep net revenue retention above 114% by expanding cards, travel, and control workflows.

children.name: Card attach in managed spend accounts
children.description: Increase the share of controlled-spend accounts using Rydoo card workflows to 38%.

children.name: Gross processing margin per expense
children.description: Improve gross margin on processed expense volume by lowering manual handling cost per expense by 22%.

children.name: Manual review share
children.description: Reduce the share of submitted expenses requiring finance review to below 12%.

productStrategy.vision: Give finance teams a trusted, low-friction spend operating system that captures every employee spend event, enforces policy automatically, and closes cleanly into accounting.

1_year.focus: Raise mobile submission compliance and reduce manual review volume in multi-entity customers.
1_year.productTheme: Expense capture, policy automation, and card reconciliation quality

customerKPI.northStar: Monthly close cycle for employee spend

supporting: Net revenue retention in enterprise accounts

milestones: Make policy and smart-audit defaults deployable by entity
Improve card-feed matching and exception workflows
Ship cleaner ERP export mappings and tax handling

3_year.focus: Expand from expense submission into full managed business spend controls across travel, cards, reimbursement, and AP-adjacent finance work.

supporting: Card attach in managed spend accounts

milestones: Connect booking and expense policy into one trip workflow
Expand controlled card issuance and budget controls
Add invoice and supplier-spend adjacency for controllers

5_year.focus: Become the default business spend operations layer for distributed companies with policy, payment, and accounting controls built into every spend path.

customerKPI.northStar: Autonomous close rate for employee spend

businessKPI.northStar: Gross profit per managed spend account
supporting: Net revenue retention in enterprise accounts

milestones: Automate most finance reviews through explainable controls
Expand global card and travel workflows with regional policy support
Offer predictive spend optimization and negotiated supplier steering

customers.name: Travel Manager / Procurement Lead
customers.description: Travel program owner responsible for keeping employee trips bookable, policy-compliant, and cost-efficient while coordinating travel providers, approvals, duty of care expectations, and post-trip spend visibility.
careAbout: Higher in-policy booking behavior without heavy policing
Negotiated supplier utilization and lower average trip cost
Clear traveler visibility and trip approval controls
Fast resolution of itinerary or spend exceptions
One view of booked, card-paid, and reimbursed travel spend
winsThem: Travel booking ingestion tied directly to expense policy
Pre-trip approval rules that stop avoidable leakage
Traveler-friendly mobile flows and card-backed trip spend
Trip-level reporting by route, merchant, team, and policy breach
TMC and booking-platform integrations that keep data complete
theirFear: Employees booking outside policy because the compliant path is too hard
Travel spend leakage hiding across cards, reimbursements, and direct bookings

jobsToBeDone.name: Keep business travel compliant before cost is locked in
jobsToBeDone.what_it_is: Guide trip planning, approvals, booking choices, and payment methods so employees stay in policy before travel spend becomes sunk cost.
jobsToBeDone.outcome: Lower average trip cost, higher negotiated supplier use, and fewer policy exceptions reaching finance after the trip.

steps.step: Bring booking intent and itinerary data into one workflow
steps.description: Collect booking data from travel tools and direct bookings with enough structure to apply policy and approvals before the trip.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Travel Booking Hub
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Connects booking tools and itinerary data into one spend-control workflow.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Trip Policy Guardrails
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Checks route, class, rate, advance-booking, and hotel policy before approval or reimbursement.

capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Routes trip approvals by manager, entity, threshold, and travel type.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Budget and Cost Center Controls
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Checks trip spend against team and project budget limits.

steps.step: Keep booked and spent travel connected
steps.description: Make sure itinerary, card, and receipt data converge so post-trip review can focus on true exceptions.

capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Provides controlled payment rails for travel-related spend.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Card Transaction Reconciliation
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Connects trip-related card spend back to booked trips and traveler evidence.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Travel Feed and TMC Connectors
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Imports booking and itinerary updates from travel providers.

customers.name: Traveling Employee / Team Manager
customers.description: Frequent traveler or manager who needs to pay, capture, justify, approve, and submit business expenses quickly without learning finance rules or waiting on slow back-office workflows.
careAbout: Fast receipt capture and low admin effort
Knowing what is in policy before spending
Simple handling of mileage, per diem, and mixed trip costs
Using cards and mobile workflows instead of collecting paperwork
winsThem: A mobile-first flow that turns receipts into ready-to-submit expenses in seconds
Clear policy hints before submission and booking
Card transactions that appear automatically and only need receipt confirmation
Transparent status from submission to reimbursement
theirFear: Having to reconstruct trips and expenses long after the fact
Getting blocked on reimbursement because policy rules were unclear
Managers spending too much time on low-value approvals

jobsToBeDone.name: Submit expenses while the trip is still fresh
jobsToBeDone.what_it_is: Capture receipts, mileage, and trip-related spend as it happens so expense reporting becomes a lightweight part of travel rather than a post-trip chore.
jobsToBeDone.outcome: Less time spent on admin, faster approvals, and fewer rejected or incomplete claims.

top.description: Increase the share of active users who submit at least one expense or approval action each month to 78%.

children.name: Mobile monthly active usage
children.description: Increase monthly active mobile usage among licensed submitters to 82%.

children.name: Approval action completion rate
children.description: Increase the share of opened approval tasks completed by managers to 92%.

children.name: Expense user seat retention
children.description: Keep annual seat retention above 95% in customers with mobile submission enabled.

children.name: Share of claims submitted on mobile
children.description: Increase the share of expenses submitted through mobile workflows to 72%.

productStrategy.vision: Make business spending as easy to handle as a consumer wallet while keeping company policy and finance controls invisible until they are actually needed.

1_year.focus: Reduce submission and approval friction across mobile-first travel and expense flows.

supporting: Share of claims submitted on mobile

milestones: Improve receipt extraction and form prefill
Reduce taps required for mobile submission and approval
Expose clearer reimbursement and policy status

3_year.focus: Blend booking, card, receipt, and reimbursement actions into one continuous spend journey for employees and managers.
milestones: Auto-create more expenses from card and itinerary events
Enable manager approvals with richer risk summaries
Improve cross-device continuity

5_year.focus: Remove most manual expense reporting by turning spend evidence, policy, and payout steps into background automation.

businessKPI.northStar: Gross profit per active mobile user

milestones: Automate most receipt, card, and trip matching without user intervention
Shift managers from line-item review to exception review
Make reimbursement timing predictable and transparent
